hostsDialog.hos.info: By default, if a request host is set in the XRAY configuration, it will be used. However, if needed, you can set a custom request host here.
hostsDialog.multiHost: To set multiple addresses, separate them with , A random address will be selected each time.
hostsDialog.wildcard: Use * to generate a random string (works for wildcard domains)
hostsDialog.path.info: Set a path for host users, useful behind a reverse proxy.
hostsDialog.port.nfo: By default, the host uses the port declared in the inbound. You can set a custom port if traffic is redirected from a different port. For example, the server might redirect traffic from port 443 to your inbound's default port.

proxyOutbound.info: Additional outbound traffic (only in v2ray custom configuration)

hostsDialog.security.info: If this host's middleware server uses a different security layer than your inbound default, you can set a custom security layer here.
hostsDialog.port.info: By default, the host uses the port declared in the inbound. You can set a custom port if traffic is redirected from a different port. For example, the server might redirect traffic from port 443 to your inbound's default port.
hostsDialog.host.info: By default, if a request host is set in the XRAY configuration, it will be used. However, if needed, you can set a custom request host here.
hostsDialog.host.multiHost: To set multiple addresses, separate them with ,  A random address will be selected each time.
hostsDialog.host.wildcard: Use * to generate a random string (works for wildcard domains)
hostsDialog.sni.info: SNI (Server Name Indication) is used to specify which hostname the client is trying to connect to. This is particularly useful when you have multiple domains pointing to the same IP address.
hostsDialog.echQueryStrategy.info: ECH query strategy. Available values: none, half, full.

hostsDialog.verifyPeerCertByName.info: Optional names that must match the peer certificate during TLS verification.
hostsDialog.echConfigList.info: Encrypted Client Hello (ECH) configuration list. This is a base64 encoded string.
hostsDialog.vlessRoute.info: Optional route value for VLESS hosts. Must be exactly 4 hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f, A-F). Leave empty to use default routing.

coreConfigModal.chooseAuthentication: Choose one authentication to use, do not mix them. Ephemeral key exchange is post-quantum safe anyway.
coreConfigModal.vlessEncryptionHint: Choose the wire format for encrypted payloads.
coreConfigModal.vlessEncryptionNativeInfo: native: Sends frames in their original layout for maximum compatibility.
coreConfigModal.vlessEncryptionXorpubInfo: xorpub: Obfuscates the public-key section for moderate camouflage.
coreConfigModal.vlessEncryptionRandomInfo: random: Wraps everything in random bytes similar to VMess/ShadowSocks for highest disguise.
coreConfigModal.vlessPaddingHint: Use probability-min-max blocks separated by dots, e.g. 100-111-1111.75-0-111.50-0-3333.
coreConfigModal.vlessClientPaddingHint: Padding blocks alternate with delay blocks; disable to rely on core defaults.
coreConfigModal.discardChangesDescription: Your unsaved kernel configuration changes will be lost if you close this editor.

coreEditor.discardDesc: You have unsaved edits. Leave without saving?

parityUi.jsonMapEmpty: No fields yet. Click + to add one.

wg.interfaceBlurb: WireGuard interface settings and key material for this core.

field.curvePreferencesHint: Only documented Xray curve names can be added. Unsupported values from JSON are shown above and can be removed.

sockopt.enableHint: Low-level TCP/IP and routing controls on stream settings. Leave off unless you know you need this.
sockopt.domainStrategyHint: How outbound dials resolve domain targets. Misused with built-in DNS and routing, this can cause loops — see Xray docs.
sockopt.acceptProxyProtocolHint: Inbound only. Peer must send PROXY v1/v2 immediately after TCP connect.
sockopt.dialerProxyHint: Use another outbound to establish this connection (advanced chaining).
sockopt.markHint: Linux only, non-zero marks packets. Requires CAP_NET_ADMIN.
sockopt.tcpCongestionHint: Linux only (e.g. bbr, cubic). Empty uses OS default.
sockopt.v6onlyHint: Linux only: :: accepts only IPv6 when enabled.
sockopt.happyEyeballsHint: Race IPv4/IPv6 connects for domains. Requires domain strategy other than AsIs.
sockopt.customSockoptHint: Advanced: raw socket options (Linux / Windows / Darwin). Must be a JSON array of objects.
sockopt.customSockoptInvalid: customSockopt must be valid JSON array.

inbound.keepAtLeastOne: At least one inbound is required.
inbound.unmanagedHint: This inbound is managed as raw JSON in Advanced.

basics.title: Basic settings
basics.desc: Set protocol and endpoint fields first, then expand advanced sections if needed.

inbound.vlessFlowVisionRequiresTls: XTLS Vision requires TCP with TLS/REALITY, xHTTP with TLS, or VLESS Encryption.

tlsFallbacks.title: TLS fallbacks
tlsFallbacks.hint: For VLESS or Trojan with TCP and TLS. Xray matches SNI, ALPN, and path on the first bytes after TLS, then forwards to dest (port, host:port, or UDS path). Rows without dest are ignored.
tlsFallbacks.name: SNI (name)

ech.sectionHint: Configure ECH server keys, config list, and force-query behavior. Generate fills key material according to the usage option.

inbound.tlsCertificateServeOnNodeHint: Certificate files are only used on the node; the server will not read them for SNI.

tlsCertificates.emptyHint: No certificates yet. Click + to add one.

inbound.vlessEncryptionStringPlaceholder: Full client encryption string (from generate or paste)

validation.peerRequired: At least one peer is required.
validation.peerPublicKeyRequired: Each peer must have a public key.

tcp.stringMapNoHeaders: No headers yet. Click + to add one.

validation.portRange: Port must be a port number or list like 443, 1000-2000,444.
validation.destinationPortRange: Destination port must be a whole number from 0 to 65535.
validation.realityServerNamesRequired: REALITY requires at least one server name.
validation.realityServerNamesFormat: REALITY server names must be a list of non-empty strings.
validation.realityServerNamesNoEmpty: Each REALITY server name must be non-empty; remove blank lines or extra commas.

inbound.discardDraftDescription: This inbound is not in the list yet. Close without adding it will discard your changes.
inbound.discardEditDescription: Your modifications to this inbound will be lost if you close now.
inbound.finishCurrentDescription: Add it to the list, or close the dialog and discard the draft, before starting another inbound.

outbound.keepAtLeastOne: At least one outbound is required.
outbound.dialogDescAdd: Adjust the draft outbound, then add it to the list. Changes apply to the draft until you confirm.
outbound.dialogDescEdit: Edits apply to the selected item in the list.
outbound.unmanagedHint: This outbound is managed as raw JSON in Advanced.
outbound.flowVisionRequiresTls: Vision flows require stream security TLS or REALITY (not “none”).
outbound.discardDraftDescription: This outbound is not in the list yet. Closing without adding will discard your changes.
outbound.discardEditDescription: Your modifications to this outbound will be lost if you close now.
outbound.finishCurrentDescription: Add it to the list, or close the dialog and discard the draft, before starting another outbound.
outbound.uriImportFailed: Could not parse that share link.
outbound.jsonInvalid: Fix JSON errors before leaving the JSON tab.

latency.title: Test outbound latency
latency.empty: Select a connected node and run the test.
latency.emptyAll: Select a connected node and test all outbounds.
latency.errorDescription: Unable to retrieve outbound latency for this node.
latency.noConnectedNodesForCore: No connected nodes are using this core.
latency.saveCoreFirst: Save this core and connect a node to it before testing outbound latency.

freedom.blurb: Freedom forwards traffic as-is. Optional: domain strategy, redirect, PROXY protocol, TCP fragment, UDP noises, and final rules (see Xray docs). Use the JSON tab for uncommon shapes.
freedom.finalRulesHint: Applied at Freedom’s final outbound stage. IP: one CIDR or geoip tag per line.

loopback.hint: Traffic from this outbound is re-injected into routing using this inbound tag.

dns.blurb: DNS outbound handles plaintext DNS from routing. Optional rewrites (transport, address, port) and ordered rules (action, qtype, domain). Use the JSON tab for edge cases.
dns.rulesHint: Conditions combine with AND. Domain: routing-style matchers, one per line. Qtype: numeric type, range, or comma list (see IANA / Xray docs).

ech.sectionHint: Configure ECH client keys and behavior.

mux.enabledHint: Multiplex multiple streams over one connection

proxy.transportLayerHint: Also proxy the transport layer stream

validation.tagRequired: Enter a tag for this outbound.
validation.inboundTagRequired: Inbound tag is required for loopback.

routing.searchPlaceholder: Search by tags, domain, port, network…
routing.dialogDescAdd: Adjust the draft rule, then add it to the list. Changes apply to the draft until you confirm.
routing.dialogDescEdit: Edits apply to the selected rule in the list.
routing.discardDraftDescription: This rule is not in the list yet. Closing without adding will discard your changes.
routing.finishCurrentDescription: Add it to the list, or close the dialog and discard the draft, before starting another rule.

validation.requiresOutboundOrBalancer: Set an outbound tag or a balancer tag so the rule knows where to send traffic.
validation.unknownOutboundTag: Outbound tag "{{tag}}" is not defined on this profile.
validation.unknownBalancerTag: Balancer tag "{{tag}}" is not defined under routing.balancers.
validation.portRange: Port must be a port number or list like 443, 1000-2000,444.

routing.routingActionHint: Choose one target for matched traffic. When both are set, outboundTag takes precedence over balancerTag.
routing.matchConditionsHint: All specified conditions must be satisfied for this rule to apply.
routing.identificationHint: Optional label for debugging. When set, matched rules are logged at Info level.

webhook.urlHint: HTTP(S) URL or a Unix socket path. Use sock:/path for the root, sock:/path:/endpoint to target a specific endpoint.
webhook.deduplicationHint: Time window for ignoring duplicate events. Leave empty to disable.
webhook.headersEmpty: No headers. Click + to add one.

balancer.searchPlaceholder: Search by tag, selector outbounds, strategy…
balancer.dialogDescAdd: Adjust the draft balancer, then add it to the list. Changes apply to the draft until you confirm.
balancer.dialogDescEdit: Edits apply to the selected item in the list.
balancer.discardDraftDescription: This balancer is not in the list yet. Closing without adding will discard your changes.
balancer.finishCurrentDescription: Add it to the list, or close the dialog and discard the draft, before starting another balancer.
balancer.observationRequiredWithBalancers: At least one observation source must stay enabled while this profile has balancers.
balancer.observatoryRequiresLeastPing: Observatory needs at least one balancer with the leastPing strategy. Use Burst observatory until then.
balancer.selectorEmpty: Add outbound tags to participate in this balancer.
balancer.noObservationSources: Enable an observation source here when using leastPing or leastLoad, or when random/roundRobin should filter unavailable outbounds.

dns.hint: Edit the full DNS object as JSON in the dialog.
dns.dialogDesc: Apply changes on blur. Close when finished.
dns.queryStrategyHint: Overall strategy to resolve domain names.
dns.serverSearchPlaceholder: Search by address, tag, domain, strategy…
dns.discardDraftDescription: This server is not in the list yet. Closing without adding will discard your changes.
dns.finishCurrentTitle: Finish the current DNS server first
dns.finishCurrentDescription: Add it to the list, or close the dialog and discard the draft, before starting another DNS server.

server.skipFallbackHint: Skip this server during DNS fallback queries.

host.duplicateDomain: A mapping for that domain already exists.

host.addressesPlaceholder: Add IP or domain (one or more)

advanced.autoApplyHint: Valid JSON is merged into the draft automatically after you stop typing.
advanced.diffModeHint: Green and red lines show additions and removals compared to the last saved version.

bulk.title: Bulk
bulk.createUsersDesc: Create multiple users at once from a template
bulk.wireguardPeerIpsDesc: Reallocate peer IPs from the global pool
bulk.replaceAllPeerIpsHint: When enabled, every affected user gets a new peer IP from the pool. When disabled, only invalid or missing peer IPs are updated.
bulk.proxySettingsDesc: Configure the proxy settings to apply
bulk.applyToProxyDesc: Select the groups, users, or admins you want to apply proxy settings to
bulk.expiredFilterHint: Optional. Narrow results by the user's expire date.
bulk.expireDateDesc: Select the expiration date to apply
bulk.applyToExpireDesc: Select the groups, users, or admins you want to apply expiration date to
bulk.dataLimitDesc: Set the data limit in GB to add or subtract
bulk.groupsOperationDesc: Select whether to add or remove groups from users.
bulk.subtractDataLimitHint: If the amount to subtract is greater than a user's current limit, that user will not be counted.
bulk.applyToDataLimitDesc: Select the groups, users, or admins you want to apply data limit to
bulk.dataLimitRequired: Please enter a valid data limit.
bulk.applyDataLimitDescription: Apply the selected data limit to the selected groups, users, and admins.
bulk.confirmApplyDataLimitDescription: Are you sure you want to apply data limit of {{dataLimit}} GB to {{totalTargets}} target(s)? This action will update the data limit for all selected groups, users, and admins.
bulk.proxySettingsRequired: Please select at least one proxy setting (Flow or Method).
bulk.applyProxyDescription: Apply the selected proxy settings to the selected groups, users, and admins.
bulk.confirmApplyProxyDescription: Are you sure you want to apply proxy settings to {{totalTargets}} target(s)? This action will update the proxy settings for all selected groups, users, and admins.
bulk.noTargetsSelected: Please select at least one target.
bulk.groupsDescShort: Select the groups you want to apply
bulk.applyToDesc: Select the user or admin you want to apply settings
bulk.confirmApplyExpireDescriptionAll: Are you sure you want to apply the expiration date {{expireDate}} to ALL users, admins, and groups? This will update the expiration date for everyone.
bulk.confirmApplyExpireDescription: Are you sure you want to apply the expiration date {{expireDate}} to {{totalTargets}} target(s)? This will update the expiration date for all selected groups, users, and admins.
bulk.confirmApplyDataLimitDescriptionAll: Are you sure you want to apply the data limit of {{dataLimit}} GB to ALL users, admins, and groups? This will update the data limit for everyone.
bulk.configureSettingsDesc: Set up the parameters for your bulk operation
bulk.selectTargetsDesc: Choose who to apply the operation to
bulk.reviewAndApplyDesc: Review your settings and apply the bulk operation
bulk.applyToAllWarning: This operation will apply to ALL users, admins, and groups in the system.
bulk.noSelectionInfo: No targets selected. This operation will apply to ALL users, admins, and groups in the system.
bulk.confirmApplyAll: Are you sure you want to apply this operation to ALL users, admins, and groups?
bulk.confirmApplyTargets: Are you sure you want to apply this operation to {{count}} target(s)?
bulk.addGroupsInfoStep2: Groups selected in Step 1 will be added to the selected users
bulk.removeGroupsInfoStep2: Groups selected in Step 1 will be removed from users. Select groups in "Has Groups" to filter which users are affected
bulk.removeGroupsHint: Select at least one group in "Has Groups" to filter users
bulk.hasGroupsDescription: Users must have these groups to be affected
bulk.hasGroupsDescriptionAdd: Filter users who have these groups
bulk.applyTemplateDesc: Apply a user template to selected users
bulk.applyTemplateError: Failed to apply template to selected users
bulk.applyTemplatePrompt: Are you sure you want to apply the template {{templateName}} to {{count}} selected user(s)?
bulk.confirmApplyProxyDescriptionAll: Are you sure you want to apply the proxy settings to ALL users, admins, and groups? This will update the proxy settings for everyone.
bulk.confirmApplyGroupsDescriptionAll: Are you sure you want to apply the group changes to ALL users, admins, and groups? This will update the groups for everyone.
bulk.confirmApplyGroupsDescription: Are you sure you want to apply the group changes to {{totalTargets}} target(s)? This will update the groups for all selected users and admins.

advanceSearch.title: Advanced Search
advanceSearch.searchModeDescription: Choose how the main search field should be interpreted.
advanceSearch.byProtocolDescription: Search protocol details and configuration data.
advanceSearch.filtersSectionDescription: Use one or more filters to narrow the list.
advanceSearch.showCreatedByDescription: Adds the creator column to the users table.
advanceSearch.showSelectionCheckboxDescription: Shows row checkboxes for bulk selection.
advanceSearch.statusDescription: Leave on All to include every status.
advanceSearch.dataLimitDescription: Filter users by data-limit range in gigabytes.
advanceSearch.noDataLimit: Only users with no data limit

advanceSearch.onlineDescription: Shows users active in the current online window.
advanceSearch.noExpire: Only users with no expire date
advanceSearch.noExpireDescription: Shows users whose account has no expire date.
advanceSearch.displaySectionDescription: These options only change how the user list is shown.

hosts.filters.userStatusDescription: Matches hosts by configured user statuses (active, on-hold, limited, etc).
hosts.filters.hostStateDescription: Filters whether the host itself is enabled or disabled.
